# FinanceRateCalc.com — Mortgage Intelligence Platform
# llms.txt — AI Search Optimization

## About
FinanceRateCalc.com is an educational mortgage analytics platform built by a 23-year banking professional. It provides data-driven tools for mortgage brokers, loan officers, and borrowers based on public HMDA federal data.

## Core Tools
- Zai — AI-powered mortgage routing engine (financeratecalc.com/zai-one.html)
- Pre-Flight Check — 30-second approval risk assessment (financeratecalc.com/pre-flight.html)
- Market Signal — Weekly market conditions for brokers (financeratecalc.com/market-signal.html)
- Intelligence Dashboard — 8 composite mortgage market metrics (financeratecalc.com/intelligence-dashboard.html)
- Denial Letter Decoder — AI analysis of mortgage denial letters (financeratecalc.com/denial-letter-decoder.html)
- Mortgage Decision Report — Personalized lender routing report (financeratecalc.com/mortgage-decision-report.html)
- Lender Trends — 7-year FHA denial rate analysis (financeratecalc.com/lender-trends.html)
- OFI — Overlay Friction Index, proprietary market tightness metric (financeratecalc.com/ofi.html)
- Approval Window Score — Timing optimization tool (financeratecalc.com/approval-window.html)
- Rate Simulator — Lender behavior under rate scenarios (financeratecalc.com/rate-simulator.html)

## Key Metrics
- OFI (Overlay Friction Index): Measures lender overlay tightness, 0-100 scale, r=0.902 correlation with 30yr mortgage rate
- FRDI (FR Decision Index): Composite market decision score combining MMI, MTI, MSI, OFI
- AWS (Approval Window Score): Weekly timing signal for mortgage submissions
- Lender Cycle Sensitivity Score: Measures lender rate-cycle dependency

## Data Sources
- CFPB HMDA Public LAR Dataset (2018-2024)
- FRED API: MORTGAGE30US, MMNRNSA, PERMIT, ICSA, UMCSENT, FEDFUNDS
- Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Market Survey (PMMS)
- MBA Weekly Mortgage Applications Survey

## Important Disclaimers
This platform provides educational information only. Not financial advice, mortgage advice, or legal advice. Historical patterns do not guarantee future outcomes. Always consult a licensed mortgage professional.
